CCR.Complaint: Bellows not cycling; tidal volume low during pre-use check.
CCR.Cause: Circuit misconnected and bellows improperly seated.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected circuit connections, reseated bellows, verified normal cycling with test ventilation.

helpfulDetails: No unusual sounds or leaks noted
Final device status: ready for service after test ventilation

title: GE Healthcare Aespire Anesthesia Machine - Flow Sensor Calibration Fault
description: Identifies causes of inaccurate flow readings or calibration errors, including sensor misalignment, obstruction, or software issues, before patient exposure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is connected to the machine.
Move the patient to an alternate anesthesia workstation if ventilation is required.

steps.title: Check Power and Startup
steps.instructions:
Verify the machine powers on without errors.
Ensure the battery is charged or the device is connected to a stable outlet.
Expected outcome: No startup errors, normal boot sequence.

steps.title: Inspect Flow Sensor Connections
steps.instructions:
Check that all gas sampling lines, flow sensor connectors, and circuit tubing are securely connected.
Look for kinks, disconnections, or moisture in the lines.
Expected outcome: Proper connection, no visible damage.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Flow Sensor
steps.instructions:
Inspect the sensor for physical damage, contamination, or moisture.
Clean or dry the sensor if needed using manufacturer-approved methods.
Expected outcome: Sensor is clean and unobstructed.

steps.title: Run Machine Self-Test / Calibration
steps.instructions:
Access the Aespire calibration menu.
Initiate automatic flow sensor calibration following on-screen prompts.
Observe for error messages or abnormal readings.
Expected outcome: Calibration completes successfully without error.

steps.title: Check Gas Supply and Pressure
steps.instructions:
Ensure all required gases are available and pressures are within normal ranges.
Low or fluctuating gas pressure can trigger calibration errors.
Expected outcome: Gas pressures stable, no error alarms.

steps.title: Swap Components if Available
steps.instructions:
If another compatible flow sensor or circuit is available, swap to test whether the error persists.
Expected outcome: If error resolves, original sensor may need repair or replacement.

CCR.Complaint: Flow sensor calibration fails during pre-use checkout.
CCR.Cause: Sensor obstructed by moisture or connector misaligned; self-test fails.
CCR.Resolution: Checked connections, cleaned sensor, attempted calibration; issue persists, machine removed from service and labeled Out of Service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: GE Healthcare Aespire Anesthesia Machine - Display Board or AGM Module Errors
description: Identifies causes for display malfunctions, touchscreen errors, or AGM module communication issues before patient exposure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is connected to the machine.
Move the patient to an alternate anesthesia workstation if ventilation is required.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions:
Verify the machine is plugged into a working, grounded outlet.
Confirm battery (if present) is charged.
Expected Outcome: Display powers on and remains stable.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions:
Ensure all cables to the display board and AGM module are secure.
Check for visible damage or loose connectors.
Expected Outcome: Proper communication between modules; error may clear.

steps.title: Restart the Machine
steps.instructions:
Power cycle the anesthesia machine after confirming no patient is attached.
Expected Outcome: Temporary software or module glitches may resolve.

steps.title: Check for Software Alerts or Messages
steps.instructions:
Review on-screen diagnostics or error logs for AGM module faults.
Expected Outcome: Identify whether issue is software or hardware related.

steps.title: Swap AGM Module or Display (If Available)
steps.instructions:
If the machine has a spare AGM module or display, swap to test.
Expected Outcome: Confirms whether the fault is in the module/display.

CCR.Complaint: Display shows AGM module error during routine pre-use check.
CCR.Cause: Module communication failure confirmed; cables and power verified.
CCR.Resolution: Machine removed from service; sent for repair; alternate workstation used for patient.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: GE Healthcare LOGIQ P Series Ultrasound System - Probe Not Recognized or Connection Error
description: Troubleshooting probes not being detected due to connection issues, port faults, or system recognition errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the probe from the patient and move to another functioning system if needed. Never troubleshoot probe recognition on an active patient.

steps.title: Verify System Power and Boot State
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is fully powered on and has completed boot-up. Partial boot or software lag can prevent probe detection.

steps.title: Check Probe Connection at the Port
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the probe. Ensure connector is seated and inspect for bent pins, debris, or fluid.

steps.title: Try a Different Probe Port
steps.instructions: Move the probe to another port to determine if the issue is port-specific.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Probe
steps.instructions: Use a working probe to determine if the issue is probe-related or system-related.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Cable and Strain Relief
steps.instructions: Check cable for cuts, kinks, soft spots, or loose strain relief.

steps.title: Check System Probe Selection / Activation
steps.instructions: Verify probe appears in UI and correct probe type is selected if required.

steps.title: Reboot the Ultrasound System
steps.instructions: Power cycle the system to clear communication faults.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages or Alerts
steps.instructions: Observe and document any probe or port-related error messages.

steps.title: Inspect for Environmental or Contamination Issues
steps.instructions: Check for gel, fluid, or debris in ports and clean appropriately.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound probe not recognized when connected to system.
CCR.Cause: Faulty probe connector identified after testing with known good probe.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defective probe from service and sent for repair; system verified operational with alternate probe.

title: GE Healthcare LOGIQ P Series Ultrasound System - Image Dropout or Quality Degradation
description: Addresses poor image quality or intermittent dropout caused by probe issues, settings, connections, or environmental interference before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the probe from the patient and transition to another functioning system if imaging is needed. Do not troubleshoot during active clinical use.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe and Cable
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage such as cracks, fluid intrusion, or frayed cable shielding. Damaged probes commonly cause dropout or artifact. If damage is found, remove probe from service.

steps.title: Reseat the Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the probe to the system port. Poor seating can cause intermittent signal loss. Confirm the locking mechanism is fully engaged.

steps.title: Try a Different Probe (If Available)
steps.instructions: Connect a known working probe to the same port. If image quality returns, the original probe is likely faulty. If issue persists, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Test the Original Probe on Another System
steps.instructions: If possible, connect the probe to a different compatible ultrasound system. Confirms whether the issue follows the probe or stays with the system.

steps.title: Check Imaging Presets and Settings
steps.instructions: Verify gain, depth, frequency, and focus settings are appropriate. Incorrect settings can mimic dropout or poor image quality. Reset to default preset if unsure.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Port for Debris or Damage
steps.instructions: Look for bent pins, debris, or fluid in the connector. Contamination can disrupt signal transmission. Clean only per manufacturer-approved methods.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure cables are not routed near strong electrical sources (e.g., power cords, other equipment). Electrical interference can degrade signal quality.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a full system reboot. Clears temporary software or communication issues.

steps.title: Observe for Consistency of Issue
steps.instructions: Determine if dropout is constant or intermittent. Constant issues are likely hardware-related. Intermittent issues are likely connection or cable-related. If the issue resolves at any step, return the device to service and document findings.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound system experiencing intermittent image dropout during scanning.
CCR.Cause: Faulty probe cable causing intermittent signal loss during movement.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced defective probe and verified normal image quality with functional testing.

title: GE Healthcare LOGIQ P Series Ultrasound System - System Fails to Boot or Freezes
description: Addresses system startup failure or freezing caused by power issues, peripherals, software lockups, or environmental factors before assuming internal hardware failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in active clinical use. Move the patient to another functioning ultrasound system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions:
Check that the system is plugged into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Test the outlet with another device.
Expected Outcome: System receives stable power. No power may indicate outlet or power cord issue.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions:
Ensure the power cord is fully seated at both the wall and system. Look for damage or loose connections.
Expected Outcome: Secure connection eliminates intermittent power or startup failure.

steps.title: Check Power Indicator Lights
steps.instructions:
Observe system LEDs when attempting to power on.
No lights: likely power input issue
Lights but no boot: possible system hang or internal issue
Why it matters: Helps differentiate between power vs. system-level failure.

steps.title: Perform a Hard Power Reset
steps.instructions:
Turn the system off, unplug from power, wait 60 seconds, then reconnect and power on.
Expected Outcome: Clears temporary faults or system lockups.

steps.title: Disconnect External Peripherals
steps.instructions:
Remove all probes, USB devices, printers, and network cables. Attempt to boot with minimal configuration.
Expected Outcome: System boots normally if a peripheral was causing the freeze.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Vent Blockage
steps.instructions:
Inspect vents and fans for dust buildup or obstruction. Ensure adequate airflow around the unit.
Expected Outcome: Overheating conditions resolved; system may boot normally after cooling.

steps.title: Observe Boot Behavior
steps.instructions:
Watch for manufacturer logo appearing and freezing, error messages, repeated restart cycles.
Why it matters: Provides clues to software vs. hardware failure.

steps.title: Attempt Alternate Power Cycle
steps.instructions:
If freezing occurs during use, perform a controlled shutdown (if possible), then restart. If unresponsive, perform a forced shutdown and restart once.
Expected Outcome: Temporary software freeze may clear.

steps.title: Check Battery (If Equipped)
steps.instructions:
If the unit has an internal battery or UPS function, verify it is not causing startup issues.
Expected Outcome: Faulty battery may prevent proper boot.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound system would not boot and froze on startup screen.
CCR.Cause: System freeze persisted after power reset; no external peripherals connected; likely internal hardware or software fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service and sent for further evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Boot screen behavior or error messages

title: GE Healthcare LOGIQ P Series Ultrasound System - Touchscreen or Control Panel Unresponsive
description: Addresses unresponsive touchscreen or control panel caused by calibration drift, connection issues, software lockup, or environmental interference before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in active clinical use. Move the patient to another functioning ultrasound if imaging is required. Why: Avoids patient risk and ensures uninterrupted care.

steps.title: Check System Power
steps.instructions: Verify the system is properly plugged in and powered on. Confirm battery (if present) is charged. Expected Outcome: Power LED or boot sequence visible. Resolves simple power-related unresponsiveness.

steps.title: Inspect Touchscreen and Control Panel Connections
steps.instructions: Check ribbon cables and connectors at the back of the touchscreen/control panel for secure seating. Expected Outcome: Loose cables reconnected may restore functionality.

steps.title: Clean the Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Use a lint-free cloth and manufacturer-approved cleaner to remove dirt, moisture, or residue. Expected Outcome: Unresponsive or erratic touch behavior improves.

steps.title: Check for External Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure no conductive objects or liquid spills are near the control panel. Expected Outcome: Touchscreen responds normally once interference is removed.

steps.title: Reboot the System
steps.instructions: Power down the system completely, wait 60 seconds, then power on. Expected Outcome: Software glitches or frozen processes are cleared.

steps.title: Attempt System Calibration or Reset
steps.instructions: If available, run the touchscreen calibration procedure per on-screen prompts or service menu. Expected Outcome: Touch accuracy restored. If still unresponsive, escalate.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound touchscreen and control panel not responding during imaging.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen cable slightly loose and software needed reboot.
CCR.Resolution: Secured cable connections, rebooted system, and performed touchscreen calibration. Function restored.

helpfulDetails: Touchscreen responsiveness before and after cleaning

title: GE Healthcare LOGIQ P Series Ultrasound System - DICOM / PACS Export Failure
description: Addresses failures in sending images or studies to PACS due to network issues, DICOM configuration errors, or system communication faults before assuming hardware failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is dependent on immediate image transfer. Perform troubleshooting on completed studies or with a test patient to prevent disruption.

steps.title: Check Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Verify Ethernet/Wi-Fi connections are secure and the system has network access. Ping the PACS server from the system if possible. Expected Outcome: Network responds; no packet loss. Why: DICOM export requires reliable network communication.

steps.title: Verify PACS/DICOM Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm correct AE Title, IP address, port, and DICOM node configuration in the system. Check that the PACS server is set to accept connections from this device. Expected Outcome: Settings match PACS server. Why: Incorrect configuration prevents study transfer.

steps.title: Test with Another Study or Modality
steps.instructions: Export a small test study or image set. Expected Outcome: Test study successfully exports. Why: Confirms whether the issue is study-specific or system-wide.

steps.title: Check for Software or Export Queues
steps.instructions: Look for any stuck DICOM queues or software alerts on the LOGIQ system. Restart the DICOM service or system if allowed. Expected Outcome: Queue clears, export resumes. Why: Stalled queues or software errors often block new exports.

steps.title: Review Storage Media and Permissions
steps.instructions: Ensure the system has access to local storage if the PACS workflow uses intermediate caching. Confirm sufficient disk space and proper folder permissions. Expected Outcome: No storage errors reported. Why: Insufficient storage or permission errors can block DICOM export.

steps.title: Check Firewall or Security Restrictions
steps.instructions: Confirm that firewalls, antivirus, or network policies are not blocking DICOM ports (usually TCP 104 or configured port). Expected Outcome: DICOM port allowed; connection established. Why: Network security measures may silently block DICOM transfer.

CCR.Complaint: Unable to send completed studies to PACS; export fails with network error.
CCR.Cause: Network and DICOM configuration verified; export queue cleared, but failures persist.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from PACS workflow and labeled Out of Service; sent for service evaluation.

helpfulDetails: DICOM AE Titles and IP addresses

title: GE Healthcare MAC 5500 HD Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Lead Noise or Artifact on Acquisition
description: Identifies common external causes of lead noise or signal artifact, including electrode placement, lead integrity, patient motion, and environmental interference.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm patient is stable and not undergoing a critical procedure.
Expected outcome: Safe environment to troubleshoot without affecting patient care.

steps.title: Inspect Electrodes
steps.instructions:
Check that all electrodes are properly placed, clean, and have good skin contact. Replace any dried or expired electrodes.
Expected outcome: Reduced baseline noise; proper signal capture.

steps.title: Check Lead Wires and Connections
steps.instructions:
Inspect all lead wires for frays, kinks, or loose connections. Ensure leads are fully seated in both the machine and electrodes.
Expected outcome: Elimination of intermittent signal artifacts caused by poor connections.

steps.title: Minimize Patient Motion
steps.instructions:
Ask the patient to remain still and relaxed during acquisition. Secure limbs to reduce movement.
Expected outcome: Stabilized waveform, reduced motion artifacts.

steps.title: Assess Environmental Interference
steps.instructions:
Ensure the machine is not near high-voltage equipment, mobile devices, or strong electromagnetic fields. Move or shield the EKG machine as needed.
Expected outcome: Cleaner trace without 50/60 Hz interference or erratic spikes.

steps.title: Perform Self-Test or Calibration
steps.instructions:
Run the MAC 5500 HD’s internal self-test or calibration routine to confirm lead acquisition circuits are functioning correctly.
Expected outcome: Confirmation that device electronics are operational.

steps.title: Swap Leads if Necessary
steps.instructions:
If a specific lead consistently shows noise, swap with a known good lead to isolate the problem.
Expected outcome: Determine if noise is lead-specific or system-wide.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported intermittent lead noise during EKG acquisition.
CCR.Cause: Poor electrode contact and frayed lead wire detected.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced electrodes and swapped lead wire; noise eliminated and waveform stabilized.

title: GE Healthcare MAC 5500 HD Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Printer Failure or Paper Feed Errors
description: Identifies external causes of printer or paper feed issues, including paper jams, incorrect media, mechanical blockage, or connectivity and configuration errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG procedure.
Expected outcome: Safe to inspect the printer and paper.

steps.title: Inspect Paper Supply
steps.instructions:
Verify that the correct paper type is loaded.
Ensure the paper roll is oriented properly and not torn or folded.
Expected outcome: Paper feeds smoothly without obstruction.

steps.title: Check for Paper Jams
steps.instructions:
Open the printer compartment and remove any jammed or crumpled paper.
Look for small scraps that may block the mechanism.
Expected outcome: Printer compartment is clear and ready for feeding.

steps.title: Verify Printer Door and Latch
steps.instructions:
Ensure the printer cover is fully closed and latched.
Some models prevent printing if the door is not fully engaged.
Expected outcome: Printer recognizes that the door is secure.

steps.title: Test Paper Feed Mechanism
steps.instructions:
Use the “Feed” button to advance the paper.
Observe if the roller moves smoothly without slipping or stalling.
Expected outcome: Paper advances without resistance or error messages.

steps.title: Check Printer Connectivity
steps.instructions:
Ensure the printer cable (if external) is properly connected to the EKG unit.
Confirm the printer is powered on and any indicator lights are normal.
Expected outcome: Printer is detected by the system.

steps.title: Verify System Settings
steps.instructions:
Check that the correct printer is selected in the MAC 5500 HD menu.
Confirm paper size and print mode match the installed media.
Expected outcome: System ready to print without configuration errors.

CCR.Complaint: EKG printer fails to advance paper and produces error messages.
CCR.Cause: Paper jam and misoriented media detected during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared jam, reloaded paper correctly, verified feed and printed test strip.

title: GE Healthcare MAC 5500 HD Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Network Export / EMR Connectivity Failure
description: Identifies external causes of network export or EMR connectivity failures, including cabling, network settings, IP configuration, firewall issues, or EMR interface errors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG procedure. Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Network Connection
steps.instructions: Check that the EKG is physically connected to the correct network port or Wi-Fi. Ensure cables are secure and undamaged. Expected outcome: Network link is established (link lights active).

steps.title: Check Device Network Settings
steps.instructions: Verify IP address, subnet mask, gateway, and DNS settings match hospital network requirements. Expected outcome: Device can ping the hospital network or EMR server.

steps.title: Test EMR Interface Configuration
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct EMR destination is configured in the MAC 5500 HD. Verify user credentials or node IDs if required. Expected outcome: EMR accepts connections from the device.

steps.title: Review Firewall and Security Settings
steps.instructions: Check that hospital firewall allows outbound traffic from the MAC 5500 HD to the EMR or network storage. Expected outcome: No blocked ports or denied protocols affecting export.

steps.title: Perform a Test Export
steps.instructions: Send a sample EKG report to the EMR or network location. Expected outcome: Successful transmission without errors.

steps.title: Swap or Test Network Cable / Port
steps.instructions: If export fails, try a different network cable or port. Expected outcome: Confirms if physical connection is causing the failure.

CCR.Complaint: EKG reports are not uploading to the EMR.
CCR.Cause: Network cable was loose and IP configuration did not match EMR requirements.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected cable, corrected IP settings, verified export to EMR.

title: GE Healthcare MAC 5500 HD Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Touchscreen or Keyboard Not Responding
description: Identifies common external causes of touchscreen or keyboard unresponsiveness, including power, cable connections, system freeze, calibration issues, or temporary software lockups.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG. Expected outcome: Safe environment for troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Device Power
steps.instructions: Check that the machine is fully powered on and battery (if applicable) is charged. Expected outcome: Power indicators active; device ready for input.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure keyboard, touchscreen cable (if detachable), and USB connections are secure. Expected outcome: No loose cables causing input failure.

steps.title: Perform a Soft Reset
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit by turning it off, waiting 30 seconds, and turning it back on. Expected outcome: Temporary software glitches cleared; touchscreen/keyboard responsive.

steps.title: Check for System Freeze or Error Messages
steps.instructions: Observe the display for frozen screens or error alerts. Expected outcome: Device returns to normal operation or displays actionable error code.

steps.title: Confirm Calibration (Touchscreen Only)
steps.instructions: Access calibration menu if touchscreen partially responds; recalibrate per on-screen instructions. Expected outcome: Touch input correctly recognized across screen.

steps.title: Swap Input Device (If External Keyboard Present)
steps.instructions: If using an external keyboard, test with a known good keyboard. Expected outcome: Confirms whether failure is keyboard-specific.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen and keyboard not responding during routine EKG setup.
CCR.Cause: Power confirmed, cables secure, software may be frozen; input devices tested.
CCR.Resolution: Soft reset performed; device partially responsive; sent for service evaluation.

title: GE Healthcare MAC 5500 HD Electrocardiograph (EKG) Machine - Power or Battery Issues
description: Identifies external causes of power loss or battery failure, including outlet problems, battery depletion, loose connections, or AC adapter/cable faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions:
Confirm no patient is actively undergoing an EKG.
Expected outcome: Patient safety maintained; device can be safely powered down.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Connection
steps.instructions:
Ensure the power cord is fully seated in both the device and wall outlet.
Expected outcome: Device powers on; if not, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions:
Plug a known working device into the same outlet or use a portable outlet tester.
Expected outcome: Confirm outlet provides power; if no power, address electrical supply.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Adapter
steps.instructions:
Look for visible damage, frays, or loose connectors.
Swap with a known good cord if available.
Expected outcome: Device receives power; eliminates cord as a cause.

steps.title: Verify Battery Status
steps.instructions:
Remove the battery (if hot-swappable) and check for proper seating or corrosion.
Replace with a charged battery if needed.
Expected outcome: Device powers on via battery; confirms battery functionality.

steps.title: Test Device Power-On Behavior
steps.instructions:
Attempt to power the device with AC and battery separately.
Expected outcome: Device powers on normally; any failure here indicates internal issue.

CCR.Complaint: Device failed to power on; screen remained blank.
CCR.Cause: Battery was fully discharged; AC cord was loose.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected AC cord and installed a charged battery; device powered on successfully.

title: GE Healthcare Innova Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Image Intensifier or Early Flat Panel Artifacts
description: Identifies external causes of image artifacts such as lines, shading, distortion, or noise from connections, interference, or setup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and imaging is not active. Why: Prevents unnecessary radiation exposure during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Identify Artifact Type and Pattern
steps.instructions: Observe the artifact (lines, flicker, distortion, shading, fixed vs moving). Expected Outcome: Pattern helps determine if issue is environmental, connection-related, or detector-specific.

steps.title: Check Display Monitors First
steps.instructions: Verify artifact appears on all monitors (review monitor, control room, archive images). Why: If only on one monitor, issue is display-related—not detector. Expected Outcome: Consistent artifact across displays indicates upstream issue.

steps.title: Inspect Detector / Image Intensifier Connections
steps.instructions: Check all visible cables between detector/image intensifier and system cabinet. Look for loose, damaged, or pinched cables. Expected Outcome: Secure connections may eliminate intermittent lines or signal noise.

steps.title: Inspect Detector Surface and Housing
steps.instructions: Check for dirt, contrast residue, tape, or physical obstruction on the detector or image intensifier input surface. Clean per manufacturer guidelines. Expected Outcome: Surface contamination artifacts should resolve after cleaning.

steps.title: Check for External Interference (EMI)
steps.instructions: Power down or move nearby devices (warming blankets, pumps, other imaging systems). Why: EMI can cause flickering or patterned artifacts. Expected Outcome: Artifact disappears if interference source is removed.

steps.title: Verify System Grounding and Power Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure system is connected to proper hospital-grade power and grounding. Check for recent electrical work or shared circuits. Expected Outcome: Stable power eliminates rolling or fluctuating artifacts.

steps.title: Test Different Imaging Modes / Field Sizes
steps.instructions: Switch between fluoroscopy, acquisition, and different field-of-view sizes. Why: Some artifacts appear only in specific modes or detector regions. Expected Outcome: Helps isolate detector vs processing issue.

steps.title: Check Collimator and Filter Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure collimator blades move freely and filters are properly seated. Expected Outcome: Misalignment can create edge shadows or uneven brightness.

steps.title: Perform a System Reboot
steps.instructions: Shut down the system fully and restart following proper procedure. Why: Clears temporary software or communication faults. Expected Outcome: Artifact caused by transient issues may resolve.

steps.title: Review Recent Changes
steps.instructions: Ask staff about recent events: Cleaning or fluid exposure, Equipment movement, Power interruptions, Service work. Expected Outcome: Identifying a trigger helps pinpoint cause.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported vertical lines appearing on fluoroscopy images during procedures.
CCR.Cause: Loose detector signal cable identified causing intermittent artifact.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated and secured detector cable; verified normal image quality across all modes.

title: GE Healthcare Innova Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Table Movement or Brake / Interlock Faults
description: Identifies external causes of table movement failure, including brake engagement, interlocks, control issues, or obstructions affecting safe positioning.

steps.title: Ensure Patient and Staff Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and all staff are clear. Why: Prevents injury during unintended movement or brake release.

steps.title: Verify Table Power Status
steps.instructions: Check that the table system is powered on and indicators are active. Inspect breakers or power disconnects associated with the table. Expected Outcome: Table shows normal power indicators and is responsive.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons (table-side, room, control console). Ensure they are fully released. Why: Engaged E-stop disables all movement and may trigger interlock faults. Expected Outcome: Releasing E-stop restores table control.

steps.title: Inspect Brake Status and Controls
steps.instructions: Verify if table brakes are engaged (manual or electronic brake indicators). Attempt brake release using hand control or footswitch. Expected Outcome: Audible or visible confirmation of brake release; table becomes movable.

steps.title: Test Table Movement from Multiple Controls
steps.instructions: Attempt movement using: Table-side controls, Control console, Footswitch (if applicable). Why: Helps isolate whether issue is control interface vs table system. Expected Outcome: Movement works from at least one interface if others are faulty.

steps.title: Inspect Interlocks and Safety Conditions
steps.instructions: Check for system messages indicating interlock conditions such as: Gantry position restrictions, Detector position limits, Table travel limits. Ensure all system components are in a safe and allowable position. Expected Outcome: Clearing interlock condition restores movement.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect under and around the table for: Cables or tubing, Floor obstructions, Accessories or attachments interfering with rails. Why: Even minor obstructions can prevent movement or trigger safety lockouts. Expected Outcome: Removing obstruction allows normal movement.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connections
steps.instructions: Check visible cables between table and system cabinets for: Loose connections, Pinched or damaged cables. Expected Outcome: Restoring proper connections may resolve intermittent faults.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled system shutdown and restart. Why: Clears temporary control or communication faults between table and system. Expected Outcome: Table initializes normally and movement is restored.

steps.title: Observe for Error Codes or Alarms
steps.instructions: Document any displayed error messages related to: Brake faults, Interlocks, Motion control. Why: Provides direction for escalation if needed. Expected Outcome: Clear identification of fault type.

CCR.Complaint: Table would not move and brake appeared stuck during procedure setup.
CCR.Cause: Emergency stop button engaged, causing brake lock and interlock condition.
CCR.Resolution: Released E-stop, verified brake disengagement, and confirmed full table movement from all controls.

title: GE Healthcare Innova Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Generator Exposure Faults (HV Tank / Interlocks)
description: Identifies external causes of generator exposure faults, including interlocks, HV tank conditions, and system readiness issues preventing X-ray generation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and the system is not actively exposing. Prevents unintended radiation exposure during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify System Ready State
steps.instructions: Check that the system is fully booted with no active critical errors on the console. System indicates Ready or equivalent status. Generator faults can occur if the system is not fully initialized.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Status
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons and ensure they are fully released. Releasing E-stop clears interlock-related generator faults. Engaged E-stop disables high voltage generation.

steps.title: Inspect Room and System Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify all cabinet doors, covers, and access panels are fully closed. Check collimator, detector positioning, and gantry clearances. All interlocks satisfied with no warning indicators. Open interlocks prevent HV activation for safety.

steps.title: Check Table and Positioning Interlocks
steps.instructions: Ensure table is properly locked, brakes are engaged, and no movement faults are active. No table-related interlock warnings present. Table positioning faults can inhibit generator exposure.

steps.title: Verify Footswitch and Exposure Controls
steps.instructions: Inspect footswitch connections and test alternate controls. Exposure command is recognized by the system. Faulty or disconnected switches can mimic generator failure.

steps.title: Check Generator and HV Tank Area (External Only)
steps.instructions: Inspect for unusual noises, warning lights, or oil leakage. No visible or audible abnormalities. External HV tank issues often present with alarms or physical signs.

steps.title: Verify Power Stability to Generator
steps.instructions: Check power source, UPS, and facility stability. Stable power with no recent interruptions. HV generator faults can be triggered by unstable input power.

steps.title: Review System Error Codes or Logs
steps.instructions: Document generator-related fault codes. Codes help distinguish interlock vs HV-related faults. Accurate fault codes are critical for escalation.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Restart the system properly. Temporary faults may clear after reboot. Software or communication faults can mimic generator issues.

CCR.Complaint: System unable to produce exposure; generator fault displayed during procedure setup.
CCR.Cause: All external interlocks verified functional; fault persisted indicating likely internal HV/generator issue.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service and escalated to GE service for generator evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual noise, heat, or odor noted
Final system status (in service vs out of service)

title: GE Healthcare Innova Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - C-arm positioning or collision sensor errors
description: Troubleshooting guide for C-arm movement faults or collision sensor errors caused by obstructions, sensors, or interlocks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and all staff are clear of the C-arm. Prevents injury from unintended movement during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Observe and Identify the Error Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt a small movement and note if motion is completely disabled or stops immediately. Understanding whether it is a full lockout or sensor-triggered stop helps narrow cause.

steps.title: Check for Physical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect around the C-arm, table, detector, and floor area for cables, IV poles, foot pedals, and lead shields or accessories. Remove any potential obstruction. Clearing obstructions restores movement if collision prevention was triggered.

steps.title: Inspect Collision Sensors and Bumpers
steps.instructions: Check all visible collision sensors and bumper edges on the C-arm and detector. Ensure nothing is pressing against them, look for stuck or compressed bumpers, and check for debris or damage. Sensors should be free and not physically engaged.

steps.title: Verify Detector and C-arm Clearance
steps.instructions: Ensure proper spacing between detector and table, C-arm and patient support surfaces, and any mounted accessories. Adequate clearance prevents false collision detection.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons (room, table, console) and confirm they are fully released. Movement functions restored if E-stop was engaged.

steps.title: Test Movement Using Alternate Controls
steps.instructions: Attempt to move the C-arm using main console controls, table-side controls, and handheld or foot controls if available. If one control works and another does not, the issue may be control-specific.

steps.title: Inspect Cable Routing and Strain
steps.instructions: Check visible cables connected to the C-arm and detector. Ensure cables are not pinched, overly tight, or restricting movement, and verify proper routing. Free cable movement prevents false sensor triggers.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down and restart the system following proper procedure. Temporary sensor or positioning faults may clear after reboot.

CCR.Complaint: C-arm would not move and displayed collision sensor error during positioning.
CCR.Cause: Obstruction (IV pole and cable) contacting C-arm collision sensor triggered movement lockout.
CCR.Resolution: Removed obstruction, verified sensor clearance, and confirmed normal C-arm movement in all directions.

helpfulDetails: Whether movement was fully disabled or intermittent

title: GE Healthcare Innova Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Boot or workstation communication failures
description: Identifies external causes of boot failures or workstation communication issues, including power, network, and peripheral problems preventing system initialization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and the system is not in use. Why: Prevents interruption of care and avoids unintended system movement or exposure.

steps.title: Verify System Power Status
steps.instructions: Check that all system components are powered: Operator console, Workstation, Generator cabinet, Monitors. Inspect circuit breakers and power distribution units (PDUs). Expected Outcome: All components show normal power indicators. Why: Partial power loss can cause communication failures between subsystems.

steps.title: Observe Boot Behavior and Error Messages
steps.instructions: Watch the system during startup: Note any error messages. Identify where boot process stops (BIOS, OS, application). Expected Outcome: Clear indication of failure stage. Why: Helps determine if issue is workstation-specific or system-wide.

steps.title: Check Network and Communication Cables
steps.instructions: Inspect all visible Ethernet and communication cables between: Workstation and system cabinets, Network switches, Image processing units. Look for loose, damaged, or disconnected cables. Expected Outcome: Secure connections restore communication. Why: Loose or failed network connections are a common cause of communication faults.

steps.title: Verify Network Switch and Link Indicators
steps.instructions: Check link/activity lights on switches and ports. If available, connect a known-good device or cable. Expected Outcome: Active link lights indicate proper communication. Why: No link light suggests a physical or network hardware issue.

steps.title: Disconnect Non-Essential Peripherals
steps.instructions: Remove external devices such as: USB drives, External monitors, Unnecessary accessories. Expected Outcome: System boots without peripheral conflicts. Why: Faulty peripherals can delay or prevent system initialization.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system properly and restart using manufacturer-recommended sequence. Allow full initialization time. Expected Outcome: System completes boot and establishes communication. Why: Temporary software or communication faults may clear with reboot.

steps.title: Check Time Synchronization and Network Availability
steps.instructions: Verify the system network is active and hospital network is reachable. If applicable, confirm system time is correct. Expected Outcome: System communicates properly with networked components. Why: Some systems fail to initialize if network services or time sync are unavailable.

steps.title: Test Workstation Responsiveness
steps.instructions: Check if the workstation: Displays correctly, Responds to keyboard/mouse input, Loads application software. Expected Outcome: Functional workstation confirms issue is not hardware lockup. Why: A frozen or failed workstation can appear as a communication failure.

steps.title: Check for Recent Changes
steps.instructions: Ask staff if any recent changes occurred: Network updates, Software updates, Power outages. Expected Outcome: Identifying recent changes may explain failure. Why: Environmental or configuration changes often trigger communication issues.

CCR.Complaint: System would not boot fully and workstation not communicating with generator. System stuck during boot with no workstation response.
CCR.Cause: Loose Ethernet cable between workstation and system network switch. External USB device causing boot conflict.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated cable and verified link lights; system booted and communication restored. Removed USB device and rebooted system; normal operation restored.

helpfulDetails: Network cable condition and link lights

title: GE Healthcare Optima IGS Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Flat panel detector calibration or artifact issues
description: Identifies external causes of flat panel artifacts or calibration issues, including power instability, detector condition, and connection problems affecting image quality.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and imaging is not active. Prevents unnecessary radiation exposure during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Identify Artifact Type and Behavior
steps.instructions: Review the image and determine lines, bands, or grid patterns, flickering or intermittent noise, and fixed vs. moving artifact. Pattern helps determine if issue is detector, display, or environmental.

steps.title: Verify Artifact Across All Displays
steps.instructions: Check control room monitors, table-side displays, and stored images. Artifact on one monitor only indicates display issue; artifact everywhere indicates upstream issue.

steps.title: Inspect Detector Surface and Housing
steps.instructions: Check for contamination, damage, or foreign objects and clean if needed. Debris can mimic calibration or detector defects.

steps.title: Check Detector Positioning and Mounting
steps.instructions: Ensure detector is properly seated, locked, and aligned. Misalignment can cause shading or geometric artifacts.

steps.title: Inspect Detector and System Cables
steps.instructions: Check cables for secure connections and damage. Signal issues can create artifact patterns.

steps.title: Verify System Power Stability
steps.instructions: Confirm stable power and no fluctuations. Voltage instability can affect detector readout.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Interference (EMI)
steps.instructions: Identify and remove nearby interference sources such as electrosurgical units or mobile devices.

steps.title: Perform Detector Calibration (If Accessible)
steps.instructions: Run offset and gain calibration using system interface. Calibration corrects uniformity or shading artifacts.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled reboot to clear temporary communication or processing issues.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported vertical lines and shading artifacts appearing on all fluoroscopy images.
CCR.Cause: Detector calibration drift identified after ruling out cables, contamination, and display issues.
CCR.Resolution: Performed detector calibration; artifacts resolved and image quality restored.

helpfulDetails: Artifact type and pattern (lines, shading, flicker)
Whether artifact appeared on all monitors
Final system status (operational or removed from service)

title: GE Healthcare Optima IGS Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - DICOM / Network Communication Failures
description: Identifies external causes of DICOM or network communication failures preventing image transfer, including connectivity, configuration, and server availability issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no active procedure is in progress. Why: Prevents disruption of patient care and avoids loss of critical imaging data.

steps.title: Verify System Network Status
steps.instructions: Check network indicators on the workstation and system cabinets. Look for disconnected status, warning icons, or loss of link lights. Expected Outcome: Active network connection with normal indicators. Why: Confirms whether the system is physically connected to the network.

steps.title: Inspect Ethernet and Network Cabling
steps.instructions: Check all visible network cables between workstation, system cabinets, and wall/network ports. Look for loose, damaged, or unplugged cables. Reseat connections. Expected Outcome: Secure connections restore communication if cabling was the issue.

steps.title: Test Network Port / Wall Connection
steps.instructions: If possible, move the system connection to a known working network port or test the port with another device. Expected Outcome: Verified live network port. Why: Rules out infrastructure or wall port failure.

steps.title: Confirm PACS / Server Availability
steps.instructions: Verify that the PACS or DICOM server is powered on and reachable from other hospital systems. Expected Outcome: Other modalities can send images successfully. Why: If PACS is down, the issue is not with the Optima system.

steps.title: Check DICOM Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Review system configuration for AE Title, IP address, and port number. Compare with PACS configuration. Expected Outcome: Matching configuration allows successful communication. Why: Mismatched settings are a common cause of transfer failure.

steps.title: Attempt a DICOM Send Test
steps.instructions: Send a test image or study to PACS. Observe for immediate failure, delayed queue buildup, or error messages. Expected Outcome: Successful transfer confirms system functionality. Why: Helps determine if issue is persistent or intermittent.

steps.title: Review Error Messages and Logs (User-Level)
steps.instructions: Check for visible DICOM or network-related errors on the workstation. Note any recurring codes or communication timeouts. Expected Outcome: Error pattern may indicate configuration vs. connectivity issue.

steps.title: Restart the Workstation and Network Interfaces
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled system restart. If accessible, reboot network switch or interface (with IT coordination). Expected Outcome: Temporary communication faults may clear after restart. Why: Resets network services and clears software-related issues.

steps.title: Check for Network Changes or IT Work
steps.instructions: Confirm with IT if there have been IP changes, VLAN changes, or firewall updates. Expected Outcome: Identification of recent changes that could block communication. Why: Network configuration changes frequently cause sudden failures.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported images not transferring to PACS from Optima IGS system.
CCR.Cause: Loose Ethernet connection at workstation network port causing intermittent communication failure.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated network cable, verified link lights, and confirmed successful DICOM image transfer to PACS.

title: GE Healthcare Optima IGS Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Software initialization or application crashes
description: Identifies external causes of software startup failures or application crashes, including power, peripherals, network, and workstation-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and no procedure is in progress. Why: Prevents interruption of care and avoids unsafe system behavior during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Observe Boot and Error Behavior
steps.instructions: Restart the system and watch closely: Does it fail during OS load, application launch, or post-login? Note any error messages or freeze points. Expected Outcome: Identifying failure stage helps isolate workstation vs system issue.

steps.title: Verify System Power Stability
steps.instructions: Check power to all major components: Workstation, Monitors, System cabinets. Inspect breakers, UPS (if present), and power indicators. Expected Outcome: Stable power with no flickering or unexpected restarts. Why: Power instability commonly causes crashes or incomplete initialization.

steps.title: Check Peripheral and External Device Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect all connected devices: Keyboard and mouse, USB devices (remove non-essential devices), Monitors and video cables. Expected Outcome: System stabilizes after removing problematic peripherals. Why: Faulty or conflicting peripherals can interrupt software loading.

steps.title: Inspect Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Verify Ethernet connections to: Workstation, System network switches. Check link lights and connection status. Expected Outcome: Active network connection. Why: Some applications depend on network services during initialization and may crash if unavailable.

steps.title: Confirm Server / System Dependencies Are Available
steps.instructions: Verify availability of: PACS / DICOM servers, Domain/network authentication (if applicable). Test access from another device if possible. Expected Outcome: External systems are reachable and operational. Why: Missing dependencies can cause application failures or timeouts.

steps.title: Restart the Workstation Independently (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: If the system allows, reboot only the workstation/application host. Expected Outcome: Clean restart may resolve temporary software faults. Why: Clears memory-related issues and hung processes.

steps.title: Check Disk Space and Basic System Health Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for warnings such as: Low disk space, System alerts on startup, Slow or unresponsive behavior. Expected Outcome: No critical system warnings. Why: Insufficient resources can prevent proper application loading.

steps.title: Review Error Logs or On-Screen Alerts (If Accessible)
steps.instructions: Check for visible logs or error codes presented on the console. Document exact messages. Expected Outcome: Error details help determine if issue is software or hardware-related.

steps.title: Perform Full System Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the system properly, wait 1–2 minutes, then restart all components. Expected Outcome: System initializes normally after clean restart. Why: Resets communication between subsystems and clears transient faults.

CCR.Complaint: System application crashes during startup and fails to reach ready state.
CCR.Cause: External checks completed; no power, network, or peripheral issues found—likely workstation/software fault.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service and escalated to vendor for software recovery.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual sounds, freezing, or lag
Final system status (operational vs out of service)

title: GE Healthcare Optima IGS Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - Table motion sync or position encoder errors
description: Identifies external causes of table motion sync or position errors, including obstructions, encoder misreads, cabling, and system initialization issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and all staff are clear of movement zones. Prevents injury from unintended table movement during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Observe Error Behavior and Movement Response
steps.instructions: Attempt small table movements using the control interface. Determine if the table moves, stops abruptly, or shows incorrect or drifting position values. Helps determine if issue is mechanical restriction, encoder feedback, or sync loss.

steps.title: Check for Physical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect under and around the table for cables, foot pedals, tubing, accessories, rails, or floor obstructions. Remove anything interfering with movement. Clearing obstructions may restore normal motion.

steps.title: Verify Table Lock / Brake Status
steps.instructions: Ensure all table brakes or locks are fully disengaged and not partially engaged. Confirm no lock-related error indicators. Fully released brakes allow smooth motion and proper position tracking.

steps.title: Inspect Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Status
steps.instructions: Check all E-stop buttons and ensure they are fully released. Resetting E-stop restores motion and clears interlock-related sync errors.

steps.title: Check Table Control Devices
steps.instructions: Test hand controls, foot pedals, and control console. Look for stuck buttons or intermittent response. Faulty controls can cause conflicting commands and sync errors.

steps.title: Inspect Visible Cabling to Table Base
steps.instructions: Check accessible cables between the table and system for loose, pinched, or damaged connections. Reseat connectors if accessible. Stable connections are required for accurate encoder feedback.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down and restart the system properly. Observe table initialization and homing process. Ensures position resets correctly and clears faults caused by incomplete initialization.

steps.title: Verify System Initialization Completes Fully
steps.instructions: Ensure the system reaches a full Ready state before use. Premature operation can cause sync mismatches between motion and encoder feedback.

CCR.Complaint: Table movement inconsistent with position display; system showing encoder/sync error.
CCR.Cause: No external obstruction found; issue persists after reboot and cable checks—likely internal encoder or control fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed system from service, labeled out of service, and escalated for vendor repair.

helpfulDetails: Table movement behavior (jerky, stopped, no response)

title: GE Healthcare Optima IGS Series Fluoroscopy / Interventional System - System interconnect / CAN bus communication faults
description: Identifies external causes of CAN bus or system interconnect faults, including cabling, power instability, and module communication loss affecting system operation.

steps.title: 1. Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is on the table and the system is not in use. Why: Prevents unintended motion, exposure, or system instability during troubleshooting.

steps.title: 2. Observe Error Messages and Affected Subsystems
steps.instructions: Note which components are reporting faults (table, detector, generator, console). Check for: Multiple subsystem failures; Intermittent vs constant faults. Expected Outcome: Helps determine if issue is isolated or system-wide communication failure.

steps.title: 3. Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system using proper procedure, wait 2–3 minutes, then power back on. Expected Outcome: Temporary communication faults may clear after reinitialization. Why: CAN bus faults are often triggered by startup sync issues or transient errors.

steps.title: 4. Verify Power Stability to All System Components
steps.instructions: Check power to: Workstation; System cabinets; Generator cabinet; Table. Inspect breakers, power strips, and UPS (if present). Expected Outcome: All components powered with stable indicators. Why: Communication faults commonly occur when one subsystem loses power or boots incorrectly.

steps.title: 5. Inspect External Communication and Interconnect Cables
steps.instructions: Check all visible cables between major system components: Cabinet-to-cabinet connections; Table interface cables; Detector connections. Look for: Loose or partially seated connectors; Damaged or pinched cables. Reseat connections where accessible. Expected Outcome: Restoring proper connections may re-establish communication.

steps.title: 6. Check for Recent Movement or Environmental Changes
steps.instructions: Ask if: Equipment was recently moved; Cleaning or construction occurred; Cables may have been disturbed. Expected Outcome: Identifies external cause of disconnected or stressed cabling.

steps.title: 7. Verify No Peripheral or External Device Interference
steps.instructions: Disconnect non-essential external devices (USB, network accessories, etc.). Expected Outcome: Eliminates possibility of interference or bus overload from external devices.

steps.title: 8. Check Cabinet Doors and Interlocks
steps.instructions: Ensure all system cabinet doors and panels are fully closed and latched. Expected Outcome: Closed interlocks allow normal system communication. Why: Some systems inhibit communication when safety interlocks are open.

steps.title: 9. Observe System Status Indicators and LEDs
steps.instructions: Check for abnormal indicator lights on system cabinets or modules. Expected Outcome: Identifies which module may not be communicating. Why: CAN bus faults often originate from one non-responsive node.

steps.title: 10. Attempt Subsystem Isolation (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: If a specific subsystem is identified (e.g., table or detector), power cycle only that component if safely accessible. Expected Outcome: Isolates fault to a specific module if communication is restored or remains failed.

CCR.Complaint: System displaying CAN bus communication errors with loss of multiple subsystem control.
CCR.Cause: Loose interconnect cable between system cabinets causing intermittent communication loss.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated and secured interconnect cables; system rebooted and communication restored.

helpfulDetails: Any recent movement or environmental changes

title: GE Datex-Ohmeda S5 Anesthesia Monitor - Module Not Recognized or Communication Errors
description: Troubleshooting modules not detected, intermittent communication loss, or missing parameters on the S5 anesthesia monitor.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the module is required for active monitoring, transition the patient to alternate monitoring before troubleshooting to maintain uninterrupted monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the module is not recognized or displaying communication errors and observe if it is missing, intermittent, frozen, or showing error messages.

steps.title: Check Module Seating and Physical Connection
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the module firmly, ensure it clicks into place, and try a different slot if available.

steps.title: Inspect Module and Slot Contacts
steps.instructions: Check connectors for bent pins, debris, contamination, or wear that may prevent communication.

steps.title: Swap with a Known Good Module
steps.instructions: Insert a known working module to determine if the issue is module-specific or related to the slot or monitor.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Module in Another Monitor
steps.instructions: Place the original module into a working S5 monitor to confirm whether the module or monitor is faulty.

steps.title: Check Module Compatibility and Configuration
steps.instructions: Verify the module type is supported and properly configured for the monitor system.

steps.title: Reboot the Monitor
steps.instructions: Power cycle the monitor completely to clear temporary communication or initialization issues.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Behavior
steps.instructions: Observe if movement affects detection, indicating worn connectors or internal slot issues.

CCR.Complaint: Gas module not recognized by S5 monitor; no readings displayed.
CCR.Cause: Faulty module identified after failing in multiple monitors.
CCR.Resolution: Removed module from service and sent for repair; replaced with working module.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or module error messages
Accessories or modules swapped during testing
Indicator lights or slot detection behavior
Final device status (working or removed from service)

title: GE Datex-Ohmeda S5 Anesthesia Monitor - Gas Module Sampling Line Blockage
description: Gas readings absent, inaccurate, or delayed due to blocked sampling line, water accumulation, or obstruction affecting gas module performance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If actively in use, confirm gas monitoring is available via another device or backup method. Do not rely on compromised gas readings.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Check for symptoms: No EtCO₂ or anesthetic agent readings, flat or erratic waveform, water visible in sampling line or water trap.

steps.title: Inspect the Sampling Line for Kinks or Disconnections
steps.instructions: Follow the full length of the sampling line from patient to module. Remove any kinks or compression points and ensure all connections are secure.

steps.title: Check for Moisture or Secretions in the Line
steps.instructions: Look for visible condensation, water, or patient secretions. If present, replace the sampling line.

steps.title: Inspect and Replace the Water Trap
steps.instructions: Remove and check the water trap. Empty if reusable, or replace if disposable. Ensure it is properly seated after reinstalling.

steps.title: Check Sampling Line Filter (if present)
steps.instructions: Inspect inline filters for blockage or discoloration and replace if needed.

steps.title: Verify Sampling Pump Operation
steps.instructions: Listen for the internal sampling pump and check for normal operation. Observe if waveform returns after clearing line.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Sampling Line
steps.instructions: Replace entire sampling setup (line + trap + filter) with a known good set.

CCR.Complaint: Gas readings absent and EtCO₂ waveform flat during case.
CCR.Cause: Sampling line and water trap found occluded with moisture.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced sampling line and water trap; verified normal gas readings restored.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: GE Datex-Ohmeda S5 Anesthesia Monitor - Display or touchscreen failure
description: Troubleshooting unresponsive touchscreen, blank or dim display, frozen screen, or inability to interact with monitor controls.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is actively in use, immediately transition patient monitoring to an alternate device. Expected: Patient monitoring continues without interruption. Why it matters: Loss of display or control removes visibility of critical patient parameters.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the screen is blank, dim, or frozen, if the touchscreen does not respond, and if waveforms are still updating. Expected: Clear identification of display, touchscreen, or full system issue. Why it matters: Differentiates between UI failure and full system failure.

steps.title: Check Power Status and Indicators
steps.instructions: Inspect power LED indicators, screen backlight, and audible alarms. Confirm the unit is powered on, adjust brightness if accessible, and verify AC connection. Expected: Device is powered with visible indicators. Why it matters: A dim or blank screen may be a brightness or power issue.

steps.title: Perform a Hard Reboot
steps.instructions: Power off the unit, disconnect AC power briefly, and restart the monitor. Expected: System boots normally and display functionality returns. Why it matters: Software lockups commonly cause display or touchscreen failure.

steps.title: Check External Display Conditions
steps.instructions: Inspect for glare, contamination, or physical damage. Clean the screen and reassess. Expected: Screen visibility and responsiveness improve if contamination was the cause. Why it matters: Surface interference can affect touchscreen function.

steps.title: Test Touchscreen Responsiveness
steps.instructions: Attempt to navigate menus and test multiple screen areas. Observe for partial or no response. Expected: Touch input is consistent. Why it matters: Partial response suggests digitizer failure.

steps.title: Check for Peripheral or Module Interference
steps.instructions: Disconnect non-essential accessories and restart. Expected: Display and touchscreen function normally. Why it matters: External components may cause instability.

steps.title: Observe for System Performance Issues
steps.instructions: Look for slow response, repeated lockups, or graphical artifacts. Expected: Stable system performance. Why it matters: Indicates possible internal hardware or processing issues.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor touchscreen unresponsive and unable to navigate menus during use.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen remained unresponsive after reboot; likely touchscreen digitizer failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed monitor from service and sent to Biomed shop for further evaluation and repair.

title: GE Datex-Ohmeda S5 Anesthesia Monitor - Battery or Power Supply Faults
description: Troubleshooting power loss, startup failure, shutdowns, or battery charging issues related to external power sources, cords, or battery condition.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the monitor is in use, immediately confirm continuous patient monitoring on an alternate device before proceeding.

steps.title: Verify Power Status and Indicators
steps.instructions: Check power LED status, battery icon, and any alarms to determine whether the issue is total power loss or battery-related.

steps.title: Check AC Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the power cord is firmly seated at both ends and inspect for damage or fraying.

steps.title: Test Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Plug a known working device into the same outlet to confirm it provides power.

steps.title: Verify Power Source Configuration
steps.instructions: Confirm any power strip or UPS is functioning or bypass it by plugging directly into the wall.

steps.title: Check Battery Charging Behavior
steps.instructions: Reconnect AC power and observe for charging indicators, increasing battery percentage, or alarms.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Disconnect power, remove battery if possible, wait 30 seconds, then reconnect and power on.

steps.title: Inspect for Heat, Odor, or Noise
steps.instructions: Check for burning smells, excessive heat, or unusual sounds indicating internal failure.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Power Cord
steps.instructions: Swap the power cord with a known working one to isolate cord issues.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Check for rapid drain, inability to hold charge, or aging battery if the unit only works on AC.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor would not power on and shut down when unplugged.
CCR.Cause: Failed internal battery not holding charge.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery and verified normal operation on AC and battery power.

helpfulDetails: Presence of heat, odor, or noise
Final device status (operational or sent for repair)

title: GE Datex-Ohmeda S5 Anesthesia Monitor - Network or Central Station Connectivity Loss
description: Loss of communication between monitor and central station due to cables, ports, or configuration issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient is still being monitored locally on the S5 monitor. If central station visibility is required, move the patient to a functioning monitored device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is not visible on the central station. Check for missing patient listing, network icons, and error messages.

steps.title: Check Network Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure Ethernet cable is firmly connected, undamaged, and reseated properly.

steps.title: Check Wall Port / Network Jack
steps.instructions: Verify wall connection, check activity lights, and try a known working port.

steps.title: Inspect Network Indicator Lights on Monitor
steps.instructions: Check Ethernet port LEDs for link and activity status.

steps.title: Verify Network Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm DHCP/static configuration, IP address assignment, subnet, and gateway.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor
steps.instructions: Reboot the monitor to clear temporary communication faults.

steps.title: Check Central Station Status
steps.instructions: Confirm central station is operational and other monitors are visible.

steps.title: Swap Known Good Cable
steps.instructions: Replace Ethernet cable to rule out cable failure.

CCR.Complaint: Monitor not appearing on central station; no network connectivity.
CCR.Cause: Faulty Ethernet cable with no link activity at port.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced Ethernet cable; confirmed restored communication with central station.
